claim: The best tech stack principle is 'capabilities over tools' - focus on solving problems, not optimizing tool choices
actionable: When building your agent tech stack, choose tools that solve your immediate problem first, then optimize later as you understand real constraints.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: PostgreSQL with PG Vector enables vector database functionality without needing a separate dedicated vector DB like Pinecone or Quadrant
actionable: Use PostgreSQL with PG Vector extension for RAG implementations when you also need traditional SQL database functionality.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: LLMs understand SQL much better than NoSQL query syntax like Firestore or MongoDB, making SQL the standard for AI agents
actionable: Use SQL databases for agent-driven applications because LLMs generate better queries for SQL than for NoSQL.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: Pydantic AI is better than raw LLM calls or other frameworks because it offers flexibility while still providing helpful abstractions
actionable: Use Pydantic AI as your agent framework rather than raw LLM calls or rigid frameworks to balance control and convenience.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: Pydantic AI automatically supports new protocols like MCP, A2A, and AGUI, making it future-proof for emerging AI standards
actionable: Choose Pydantic AI specifically for access to latest protocols without waiting for manual framework updates.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: LangGraph handles state management, human-in-the-loop, routing, and graph persistence for multi-agent systems better than alternatives
actionable: Use LangGraph specifically for multi-agent workflows when you need state management and human-in-the-loop capabilities.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: Arcade handles agent authorization and tool security, enabling agents to access user accounts with OAuth flows for tools like Gmail and Slack
actionable: Use Arcade when your agents need to access user accounts or require fine-grained tool permissions.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: Langfuse provides essential agent observability including token usage, cost, latency, and tool calls; it is critical for production agents
actionable: Always integrate Langfuse or similar observability tools into your agent pipeline before deploying to production.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: Docling is the best framework for extracting data from complex documents like PDFs with diagrams and Excel files
actionable: Use Docling for PDF and complex document extraction in your RAG pipelines instead of manual extraction or less capable tools.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: Firecrawl or Crawl4AI for website data extraction; use Crawl4AI for general web scraping and LLM integration
actionable: Use Crawl4AI for live website data extraction in agent workflows; use Dockling for document files.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: Mem0 for long-term memory integrates with any database including PG Vector, enabling agents to remember across sessions
actionable: Add Mem0 to any agent built with Pydantic AI to enable persistent memory without rewriting your agent architecture.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: Neo4j for graph databases and Graphiti for entity/relationship extraction make knowledge graphs practical for RAG
actionable: Use Neo4j + Graphiti for knowledge graph implementations when you need intelligent entity and relationship extraction from documents.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: Ragas provides specialized metrics for RAG evaluation like faithfulness and relevance; use it for evals beyond agent tool calling
actionable: Use Ragas for RAG-specific evaluation metrics rather than generic agent evaluation tools.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: Playwright is superior to Selenium for web automation due to better multi-browser support and being the foundation for advanced tools like Browserbase
actionable: Use Playwright instead of Selenium for web automation tasks, especially when integrating with AI agents.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: Browserbase handles browser control with anti-bot detection, records sessions, and provides visual playback for agent debugging
actionable: Use Browserbase when you need agents to control browsers against modern anti-bot systems or need session recording for verification.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: FastAPI for Python backends and Express for TypeScript/Node backends are the standard choices for AI agents
actionable: Use FastAPI if building agents in Python; use Express if building in TypeScript to align with agent framework ecosystems.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: Claude Code is considered the best AI coding assistant, though Cursor 2.0 and Codeex are catching up; start with Claude Code
actionable: Use Claude Code as your primary AI coding assistant for building agents; monitor Cursor and Codeex as viable alternatives.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: N8N is the best no-code tool for prototyping agents, validating tools, and testing system prompts before moving to code
actionable: Use N8N to prototype agent ideas and validate prompts before investing time in full code implementations.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: Render is the simplest deployment platform with YAML infrastructure-as-code, free frontend hosting, and automatic git-based deployments
actionable: Use Render for rapid agent deployment when you value simplicity over fine-grained infrastructure control.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: Docker is the industry standard for deploying all applications; fixes the 'works on my machine' problem for agent deployments
actionable: Always containerize agents with Docker before deploying to ensure reproducibility across environments.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: GitHub Actions for CI/CD is free for public repos with generous pricing for private repos and huge marketplace of automation actions
actionable: Use GitHub Actions for CI/CD pipelines to test and validate agents automatically on every commit.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: Ollama is the easiest way to run local open-source LLMs, auto-leveraging multiple GPUs without complex config
actionable: Use Ollama to run agents locally with open-source models for privacy or development without cloud API calls.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: The workflow is prototype agents in N8N, move to code with Streamlit frontend, then build full React app when ready for MVP
actionable: Follow this three-stage agent development: N8N prototype -> Streamlit codebase -> React MVP to balance speed and quality.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026

claim: Brave Search provides privacy-focused AI search without Google proxy; Perplexity provides detailed results but slower for agents
actionable: Use Brave Search for agent-driven web retrieval when privacy matters; use Perplexity for detailed research tasks.
source_title: The ONLY AI Tech Stack You Need in 2026
